commit.message:
Deploy browser demo to GitHub Pages (#14)

* feat(deploy): publish browser demo to GitHub Pages

Summary:
- add a GitHub Pages workflow that deploys the static browser demo
  from `browser-demo` on pushes to `main`
- extend the Node test suite with a guard that verifies the Pages
  workflow targets the expected directory and deploy action
- document that the current browser demo is published after merge

Rationale:
- this repo already ships a static browser surface, so Pages can host
  the current review build without introducing a frontend build step
- keeping deployment scoped to `browser-demo` preserves the existing
  local workflow while creating a visible review URL after merge

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run validate:browser-demo

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(validation): use an ephemeral browser demo port

Summary:
- choose a free local port for browser demo validation when no explicit
  port is configured
- bind the temporary static server to 127.0.0.1 so repeated runs use a
  predictable loopback target

Rationale:
- repeated local validation could time out on the fixed default port
  even though the browser demo itself was healthy
- using an ephemeral port keeps the review harness reliable without
  changing the shipped demo surface

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run validate:browser-demo

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(evolution): add governed self-evolution substrate

Summary:
- add the governed self-evolution engine, API, and native tool for skill, runbook, starter pack, and prompt pack review candidates
- enforce registered-target validation, scope-drift guardrails, and safe workspace contribution persistence with rollback behavior
- add deterministic API and runtime-eval coverage and update shipped-truth docs for Batch AS

Rationale:
- give Seraph a human-reviewed, eval-gated self-evolution path without direct in-place mutation
- fix the real review findings around unregistered file mutation, invalid draft pollution, and under-scoped prompt-pack guardrails

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/evolution.py backend/src/evolution/engine.py backend/src/extensions/workspace_package.py backend/src/runbooks/manager.py backend/src/starter_packs/manager.py backend/src/tools/self_evolution_tool.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_evolution_api.py backend/tests/test_tools_api.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_evolution_api.py tests/test_tools_api.py -q
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_eval_harness.py -q -k "governed_self_evolution_behavior or test_main_lists_available_scenarios"
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(import): delegate to worker instead of inline processing in after() (#1127)

* fix(import): delegate to worker instead of inline processing in after()

Replace kickoffImportJob with startImportJob in all 4 import route
after() callbacks (finalize, multipart upload, commit, notify-customers).

startImportJob uses a clean strategy:
- Production with worker secret: delegates to /api/import-jobs/worker
  (runs in its own request context with maxDuration=300)
- Production without secret: throws to catch misconfiguration
- Non-production (localhost): falls back to inline processing

Previously, after() always ran processImportJobById inline first, which
claims the job then does heavy I/O. On Vercel, the function timeout
kills the process mid-work, orphaning the job with no recovery path.

Also adds logger.warn in triggerImportWorker when IMPORT_JOB_WORKER_SECRET
is missing as a defensive layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(import): harden worker kickoff recovery

---------

commit.message:
feat(presence): ship cross-surface presence continuity

Summary:
- add explicit presence-surface continuity across observer, operator, activity, and cockpit surfaces
- surface ready versus attention presence states with repair and follow-up actions plus deterministic regressions
- fold the eval-harness shard fix and shipped-truth doc updates into the same Batch AP aggregate

Rationale:
- broader reach was still stranded in separate inventories instead of the shared continuity and recovery loop
- the batch needed to fix the live CI seams and truthfulness regressions before opening the aggregate PR

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/scripts/run_backend_test_shard.py backend/src/api/activity.py backend/src/api/observer.py backend/src/api/operator.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_activity_api.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py backend/tests/test_observer_api.py backend/tests/test_operator_api.py backend/tests/test_run_backend_test_shard.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_run_backend_test_shard.py -q
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_observer_api.py -q -k "surfaces_imported_reach_and_source_adapter_attention or partial_namespace_items or keeps_disabled_and_planned_surfaces_visible"
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_operator_api.py tests/test_activity_api.py -q -k "surfaces_observer_recovery_actions"
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_eval_harness.py -q -k "test_main_lists_available_scenarios"
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -c "import asyncio, json; from src.evals.harness import run_runtime_evals; summary = asyncio.run(asyncio.wait_for(run_runtime_evals(['cross_surface_continuity_behavior']), timeout=120)); print(json.dumps({'failed': summary.failed, 'details': summary.results[0].details}, indent=2))"
- cd frontend && NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-require-module npx vitest run src/components/cockpit/SeraphPresencePane.test.tsx src/components/cockpit/seraphPresence.test.ts src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx -t "does not claim the queue is clear when presence surfaces need attention|surfaces active triage for approvals, workflows, queued guardian items, and reach failures|deriveSeraphPresenceState"
- cd frontend && npm run build
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(browser): add a local dual-player motion demo

Summary:
- add a static browser MVP with file pickers, dual MP4 playback,
  synchronized viewing, and overlay controls
- compute and render a worker-backed optical-flow approximation while
  clearly documenting the single-file Insta360 split fallback
- add browser MVP docs plus repeatable unit and screenshot validation

Rationale:
- the issue needs a browser-first review surface without reopening the
  proven host extractor work or overclaiming codec motion vectors
- pair upload plus explicit blocker messaging ships a truthful demo now
  while keeping the remaining browser split/vector boundary visible
- committed Playwright validation and screenshots leave behind a
  reproducible handoff instead of an ad hoc manual check

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run validate:browser-demo

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(payout): stabilize account verification and broaden bank-code validation (#1125)

* fix: remove route segment config incompatible with cacheComponents

The dynamic/fetchCache/revalidate exports break Turbopack build when
cacheComponents is enabled. These routes already call noStore() inside
the handler, which achieves the same no-cache behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Wire VTU checkout through app payments

* fix(payout): stabilize account verification and broaden bank-code validation

Root causes fixed:
- Infinite re-verification loop: resolveAccount (full mutation object) was an
  unstable useEffect dependency, causing a new request on every render.
  Replaced with useQuery keyed by debounced (accountNumber, bankCode) pair.
- Stale verified name: raw input changes now immediately clear accountName
  via isPendingInput guard, preventing a stale name from authorising a save.
- Save guard bug: &&-logic let saves proceed while verification was pending;
  corrected to !accountName || isVerifying.
- Bank code regex: /^\d{3}$/ rejected real Paystack codes (035A, MFB50992,
  50211, 09). Replaced with bounded alphanumeric schema (min 2, max 16).

Changes:
- New usePayoutAccountVerification hook (useQuery + debounce + isolation)
- New usePaystackBanks hook (backend /api/paystack/banks, deduped by code)
- New shared paystackBankCodeSchema wired into resolve and subaccount schemas
- Rewired payout-settings.tsx to use both new hooks; removed inline effect
- Regression tests for screen, both hooks, schema, helper, and route layers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(payments): address critical VTU checkout review findings

- Webhook: log failed VTU fulfillment instead of silently returning 200
- charge-saved-card: check transaction insert error before charging card
- confirm: use atomic claim pattern (select+maybeSingle) to prevent
  double-fulfillment on concurrent confirm requests
- use-vtu-history: include userId in React Query cache key to prevent
  cross-user data leakage on logout/login

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update confirm route test for atomic claim pattern

commit.message:
fix(validation): avoid loading huge host vector json in Python (#11)

Summary:
- add compact extractor summary sidecars and have the baseline runner
  consume them before falling back to the full vector artifact
- generalize baseline report/status text so private validation writes
  truthful manifest-aware output instead of stale HEL-156 wording
- record the HEL-157 private-lane success proof in research and
  environment docs and extend unit coverage for the new path

Rationale:
- the staged private lane produced host vector artifacts above 3 GB,
  and json.loads on those files stopped the rerun before comparison
- preserving the full vector evidence while reading a compact sidecar
  keeps the extractor family and staged inputs fixed while removing
  the Python memory bottleneck on this host

Tests:
- python3 -m unittest tests.test_public_baseline
- python3 scripts/public_baseline.py run --manifest manifests/user-validation.json --progress-artifact .symphony/progress/HEL-157.json

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: migrate to 'use cache: remote' + cache homepage products (#1126)

* perf: migrate to 'use cache: remote' + cache homepage products

Upgrade all 21 'use cache' directives to 'use cache: remote' so
cached data persists in Vercel Runtime Cache across serverless
instances instead of being ephemeral per-instance.

Changes:
- Upgrade 21× 'use cache' → 'use cache: remote' across cached-data.ts,
  cached-categories.ts, cached-storefront-product-index.ts, feed-data.ts
- Add getCachedStorefrontHomeProducts() to cache homepage products
  query (was raw uncached Supabase query on every page load)
- Remove cookies()/createClient() from storefront-content.tsx
- Remove connection() from product detail page (was forcing dynamic
  rendering unnecessarily)
- Add getMerchantStrict() for cache-safe merchant lookups that throw
  on transient failure instead of returning null
- Harden getCachedFeatureSettings() to throw on query error, switch
  to maybeSingle(), update non-cache callers with try/catch
- Fix 4 cache invalidation gaps: product-index, legacy-redirect,
  merchant-feed, navigation-categories tags
- Normalize blog cache tags to lowercase (identifier + postSlug)
- Bump hono to 4.12.12 and @hono/node-server to 1.19.13 in
  pnpm.overrides (fixes 6 medium security alerts)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update lockfile for hono overrides

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(web): refresh onboarding setup flow

Summary:
- add richer onboarding step metadata, preset-driven agent bootstrap,
  and seeded first-ticket suggestions for different setup goals
- tighten onboarding UX with skip confirmation, streamlined provider
  cards, and clearer empty-state copy across key project screens
- apply small frontend fixes required by local validation, including
  org-admin settings state usage and repo-wide Prettier adjustments

Rationale:
- new projects need clearer guidance on why each onboarding step
  matters and faster ways to choose a bootstrap path
- stronger empty states and confirmation flows reduce setup mistakes
  and make the app easier to understand before any data exists
- keeping the frontend tree lint- and format-clean avoids blocking
  follow-up web CI checks on unrelated files

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.13.0/bin:$PATH make frontend-api-audit-check
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.13.0/bin:$PATH make web-install
- custom web CI command with temporary Playwright config on port 4273
  (fails in tests/e2e/iam-settings-migration.spec.ts because
  "Migration note" was not found)

fix(machinetransport): stabilize preflight failure parsing

Summary:
- emit runtime preflight classification markers on a single stream
- keep the human-readable failure message on stderr for operators
- avoid fast-exit websocket preflight races that could downgrade
  classified openase failures to transport failures

Rationale:
- the websocket command-session CombinedOutput helper does not preserve
  cross-stream line atomicity
- duplicating the OPENASE_RUNTIME_PREFLIGHT_FAILURE marker on stdout and
  stderr let the two copies interleave and break parser classification
- this was surfacing as a flaky Backend Checks failure on the ASE-102 PR

Tests:
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/infra/machinetransport -run TestRunRemoteRuntimePreflightReportsOpenASEFailureForFastExits -count=100
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/infra/machinetransport -run 'TestRunRemoteRuntimePreflightReportsOpenASEFailure|TestRunRemoteRuntimePreflightReportsOpenASEFailureForFastExits|TestRunRemoteRuntimePreflightOverWebsocketListener' -count=1
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/orchestrator -run TestRuntimeLauncherRunTickPreparesRemoteWorkspaceDirectlyFromRepositoryURL -count=1
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" OPENASE_PGTEST_SHARED_ROOT="$HOME/.cache/openase/pgtest" OPENASE_GO_TEST_PROGRESS_MODE=json make check
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" OPENASE_PGTEST_SHARED_ROOT="$HOME/.cache/openase/pgtest" OPENASE_GO_TEST_PROGRESS_MODE=json .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(repo): support file project repo URLs

Summary:
- make project repo parsing explicitly accept file:// URLs and reject
  empty file URL paths
- add file:// regression coverage for catalog APIs, workspace prepare,
  agent platform repo routes, and runtime launcher remote prepares
- document that OpenASE supports hosted providers and machine-local or
  self-hosted Git sources, with file:// resolved on the prepare machine

Rationale:
- file:// support already worked in practice, but it was not encoded as
  an intentional contract and had no clear regression signal
- explicit parser handling plus end-to-end tests protect future repo URL
  tightening from silently breaking local or self-hosted Git flows
- user-facing docs now clarify that GitHub and GitLab are optional and
  that local reachability depends on the machine performing clone/fetch

Tests:
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/domain/catalog -run TestCatalogEntityParsersAndHelpers -count=1
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/infra/workspace -run 'TestManagerPrepareClonesFileRepositoryURL|TestWorkspaceLayoutAndParserHelpers' -count=1
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/httpapi -run 'TestCatalogCRUDRoutes|TestAgentPlatformPrivilegedRoutesRequireExplicitScopes|TestAgentPlatformExpandedProjectRoutesRequireExplicitScopes' -count=1
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/orchestrator -run TestRuntimeLauncherRunTickPreparesRemoteWorkspaceDirectlyFromRepositoryURL -count=1
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" OPENASE_PGTEST_SHARED_ROOT="$HOME/.cache/openase/pgtest" OPENASE_GO_TEST_PROGRESS_MODE=json make openapi-check check build lint lint-depguard lint-architecture

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
